title: Decide: testing/CI investment level
description: Repo has no automated tests or CI. Recommend: small pytest suite for pure logic (recipe matching, barcode heuristics, defaults rules) + GitHub Actions lint+test on push. Decide scope before Phase 2.

title: Decide: backup strategy for data volumes
description: service/data (SQLite + settings.json incl. secrets), grocy/config, mealie/data are bind mounts with no backup. Decide: simple cron tar, restic, or host-level snapshots.

title: Decide: approve/reorder proposed feature phases 3-4
description: Review the Phase 3 (Mealie deepening) and Phase 4 (polish) beads and approve, drop, or reprioritize. Top suggestions: missing-ingredients->shopping-list button; Cooked-it inventory consumption; HA meal plan sensors.

title: Decide: auth posture (Pangolin vs built-in) + Mealie admin creds
description: Currently Pangolin fronts public access and built-in AUTH_PASSWORD/API key are optional. Decide the standard: rely on proxy auth, built-in auth, or both. Also change Mealie default changeme@example.com login.

title: Review: delete stale GitHub branch claude/vibrant-goldberg-2z2hy
description: History was rewritten so all commits on main are authored by Dan. The old branch still holds pre-rewrite commits; deletion was blocked from the cloud session — delete it in the GitHub UI (Branches page).

title: Review: field-test the new Mealie integration
description: docker compose --profile with-mealie up -d; change default admin creds; create API token (User Profile -> API Tokens); configure in /setup; verify Meal Plan page, Shopping page, and What-can-I-cook suggestions against real inventory.

title: Review: rotate exposed FoodAssistant API key
description: The X-API-Key starting 081acd96 appeared in chat/shell history during HA debugging. Generate a new key (openssl rand -hex 24), update /setup and homeassistant/configuration.yaml.

title: Phase 0: Discussion — review with Dan before continuing
description: Items Dan should review/decide before further feature work. Nothing downstream starts until the relevant item here is closed.

title: API key security best-practices pass
description: Audit and fix API key handling end to end: (1) never render saved secrets into setup HTML (mask, write-only fields that keep existing value when blank); (2) settings.json file permissions 0600; (3) secrets redacted from logs and error messages; (4) constant-time comparison for X-API-Key (auth_password already uses secrets.compare_digest); (5) document rotation procedure; (6) consider encrypting settings.json secrets at rest with SECRET_KEY. Overlaps 9px.4 (setup HTML echo) — implement together.
close_reason: 0600 settings.json, compare_digest for X-API-Key, error redaction, saved-key fallback for tests, rotation notes in Security pane

title: External recipe API options: TheMealDB free/premium, Spoonacular
description: Settings section for external recipe sources: TheMealDB public API (current default, key 1), TheMealDB premium (paid supporter key, more recipes + no rate limits), or Spoonacular (user API key, much larger catalog with findByIngredients endpoint). Provider-style abstraction in services/recipes_external.py: common interface find_recipes_for_ingredients + get_external_recipe; per-source client; selection + keys in settings with test buttons. Spoonacular budget note: free tier is 150 points/day — cache aggressively.
close_reason: recipe_source picker (themealdb/spoonacular/off), TheMealDB key support, Spoonacular client with caching, /setup/test/recipes

title: Expand settings: staples list and other tunables
description: Surface hardcoded tunables in the settings UI. Known candidates: pantry staples list (currently _STAPLE_TOKENS in services/mealie.py), perishable threshold days (14), expiring-soon threshold (5), suggestion counts per tier, barcode enrichment on/off per scan source, HA-facing options. Staples editor: tag-style input, persisted to settings.json, classifier reads from settings with current list as default.
close_reason: Staples editor, perishable_days, expiring_soon_days, suggest_per_tier in Settings; classifier reads from settings

title: Redo settings menu with side navigation
description: Replace the single long /setup page with a side-menu layout (Bootstrap nav-pills column + content pane): sections for Inventory (Grocy), AI Providers, Barcode/Enrichment, Recipes (Mealie + external APIs), Interface (tabs/nav), Security (auth/API keys). Keep per-section test buttons and one global save. Mobile: side menu collapses to a top pill row.
close_reason: Side-menu settings layout: Inventory/AI Providers/Recipes/Interface/Security panes, per-section tests, global save

title: User-configurable navigation: show/hide and re-arrange tabs
description: Let users choose which nav tabs are visible and their order. Store as a saveable setting (e.g. nav_tabs list in settings.json). UI: drag-to-reorder list with visibility toggles in the settings page. base.html renders the nav from the configured list instead of hardcoding.
close_reason: nav_order/nav_hidden settings + registry-rendered navbar + show/hide/reorder editor in Settings Interface pane

title: Auto-hide tabs for unconfigured services
description: Tabs whose backing service is not configured should disappear automatically: no Recipes/Meal Plan/Shopping/Cook tabs without Mealie; no vision-dependent UI without a working provider. Auto-hide combines with manual show/hide from the nav-config feature (hidden-by-config wins over configured service). Currently unconfigured Mealie pages show an info alert — replace with hidden tab + setup hint elsewhere.
close_reason: Tabs with requires=mealie auto-hide when Mealie unconfigured; manual hide combines; pages reachable by URL

title: Make What-can-I-cook its own tab
description: Move the tiered recipe suggestions (ready/staples/shopping) out of the Meal Plan sidebar into a dedicated /ui/cook page with its own nav tab. More room means: full-width tier sections, recipe images (TheMealDB provides thumbnails), expandable ingredient lists, and the add-to-mealplan action directly from a suggestion. Meal Plan page keeps a compact link/teaser to the new tab.
close_reason: Cook tab live at /ui/cook with images, collapsible ingredients, plan/save/save+list actions

title: UI & Settings overhaul
description: Top-priority batch from Dan: dedicated cook tab, configurable navigation, settings redesign with side menu and expanded options, external recipe API choices.

title: Tiered recipe recommendations + external recipe DB + shopping list in Lovelace
description: Three recommendation tiers: (1) cookable with stock only, (2) stock + common pantry staples (eggs, butter, flour...), (3) uses perishable stock but needs shopping — with add-missing-to-list button. Pull candidate recipes from TheMealDB (free API) in addition to Mealie; selecting an external recipe saves it into Mealie. Add shopping list REST sensor + Lovelace card.
notes: TheMealDB: filter.php?i=<ingredient> then lookup.php?i=<id>, test key '1' is free. Tier definitions: water ignored everywhere; staples list in services; perishable = refrigerated bucket or expiring <=14d.
close_reason: Implemented: 3-tier classifier (ready/staples/shopping) in services/mealie.py, TheMealDB client in services/recipes_external.py with import-to-Mealie, Save+list combo for external shopping-tier recipes, HA shopping summary endpoint + sensor + Lovelace tile and card.

title: Split Mealie internal vs public URL
description: Mealie authenticates only on the LAN (http://192.168.1.170:3000) but the public URL is https://mealie.korolev.link/. A single mealie_base_url is used for both API calls and browser links — links in the UI point to the internal address which is unreachable externally. Add a second setting mealie_public_url used only for href generation in templates; all httpx API calls keep using base URL. Setup wizard exposes both fields. Fallback: if public URL is empty, base URL is used for links.
acceptance_criteria: 1. mealie_public_url in config + setup wizard. 2. All template hrefs use public URL. 3. All API calls use base URL. 4. Empty public URL falls back to base URL silently.
notes: Mirrors the HA LAN-vs-Pangolin split already in project memory. mealie_base_url stays as http://192.168.1.170:3000 for API; mealie_public_url = https://mealie.korolev.link for links.
close_reason: mealie_public_url setting added; all browser links use it via settings.mealie_link_url(); API calls unchanged on base URL; setup wizard has both fields.

title: Split Mealie internal vs public URL
description: Mealie authenticates only on the LAN (http://192.168.1.170:3000) but the external URL is https://mealie.korolev.link/. Currently a single mealie_base_url is used for both API calls and browser links, so links in the UI point to the internal address. Need two settings: mealie_base_url (API/docker-internal, never opened by a browser) and mealie_public_url (used only for href generation in templates). API calls stay on the internal address; all 'Open in Mealie' links and recipe deep-links use the public URL. Setup wizard should expose both fields with a note that public URL can be left empty to fall back to base URL.
acceptance_criteria: 1. mealie_public_url added to config and setup wizard. 2. All template hrefs (Open Mealie buttons, recipe slug links) use public URL. 3. All httpx API calls use base URL. 4. Fallback: if public URL empty, base URL used for links too.
notes: Current single-URL approach works from the server but breaks browser links for users on the external network. The pattern mirrors the existing HA LAN-vs-Pangolin split already documented in project memories.

title: Stop echoing secrets into setup page HTML
description: setup.html renders API keys back as input values — anyone with page access can read them. Mask saved values (show last 4 chars) and only overwrite when a new value is entered.
close_reason: Secrets never rendered into setup HTML; has-flags only; blank=keep, __CLEAR__=erase

title: Rotate all API keys + document rotation procedure
description: FoodAssistant API key, Grocy key, and any LLM keys pasted in shared contexts. Add a short docs section on where each key lives and how to rotate.

title: Backup/restore script for data volumes
description: Per decision in Phase 0: implement chosen backup for service/data, grocy/config, mealie/data + restore test.

title: Phase 1: Hardening & security
description: Key rotation, secrets handling, backups, image pinning.

title: Recipes tab: browse, add, photo-import, and URL-import recipes
description: Add a Recipes page to the FoodAssistant UI so users never need to visit Mealie directly for day-to-day recipe work. Four capabilities in one tab: (1) Browse — searchable list of Mealie recipes with name, description, and a link to open in Mealie; (2) URL import — paste a webpage URL, backend fetches the page and sends it to the LLM to extract structured recipe data (name, description, ingredients, instructions, servings), then POSTs to Mealie API; (3) Photo import — snap or upload a photo of a handwritten or printed recipe card, vision LLM extracts the recipe fields, user can review/edit before saving to Mealie; (4) Manual add — simple form as fallback. All saves go to Mealie via its recipe create API (POST /api/recipes). The LLM extraction for photo and URL routes should reuse the existing VisionProvider / enrich_product pattern from providers/.
acceptance_criteria: 1. /ui/recipes page with nav link. 2. Recipe list loads from Mealie with search. 3. URL import: paste URL -> LLM extracts -> review form -> save to Mealie. 4. Photo import: upload/snap -> vision LLM extracts -> review form -> save to Mealie. 5. Manual add form. 6. Each saved recipe shows a confirmation with link to view in Mealie.
notes: URL import: fetch page HTML server-side (httpx), strip to readable text, send to LLM with a recipe-extraction prompt. Photo import reuses existing analyze pattern from routers/analyze.py. Mealie recipe create: POST /api/recipes with {name}, then PATCH /api/recipes/{slug} to add ingredients/instructions — or use the scrape endpoint if Mealie supports it (POST /api/recipes/create/url).
close_reason: Recipes tab live: browse/search, URL import (Mealie scraper + LLM fallback), photo import via vision LLM, manual entry, shared review editor. extract_recipe on all 4 providers.

title: HA sensors + Lovelace card for meal plan & shopping
description: REST sensors: today/tomorrow dinner, shopping item count. Lovelace: meal plan card on the food dashboard. Remember: sensors use LAN URL, not Pangolin.

title: Add missing ingredients to shopping list (one click)
description: On each What-can-I-cook suggestion, button sends unmatched ingredients to the active Mealie shopping list. Backend: diff recipe ingredients vs inventory matches (already computed in suggest_recipes).
close_reason: Implemented ahead of Phase 0 sign-off (7cc.3 field-test, 7cc.5 approval) per Dan's direct request.

title: Cooked it: consume recipe ingredients from Grocy
description: Button on a suggestion/meal-plan entry that consumes matched inventory items in Grocy (reuse consume_stock + the ingredient matcher).

title: GitHub Actions: lint + tests on push/PR
description: ruff + pytest workflow; badge in README.

title: pytest suite for pure logic
description: Cover services/mealie.suggest_recipes + _tokens, services/barcode heuristics + LLM result handling, services/defaults.apply_defaults, providers/base.parse_json_response.

title: Route + template smoke tests
description: TestClient: every GET page returns 200, Jinja templates render, setup save round-trips. Mock Grocy/Mealie with httpx MockTransport.

title: Pin Docker image versions
description: grocy, mealie, ollama all use :latest — pin to known-good tags and document upgrade procedure.

title: Phase 3: Mealie integration deepening
description: Close the loop between recipes, shopping list, and Grocy inventory.

title: Phase 2: Tests & CI
description: Automated test suite and GitHub Actions pipeline.

title: Docs pass: screenshots + HA guide refresh
description: README screenshots of inventory/mealplan/shopping; verify homeassistant/ docs match current sensor set.

title: Separate internal vs public URLs for Grocy/Mealie
description: API calls need the LAN/docker address; browser links want the public one (Pangolin). Add optional *_public_url settings used only for href generation.

title: PWA manifest + icons for phone use
description: Add manifest.json, icons, theme color so the UI installs to a home screen for quick scanning.

title: Barcode scan auto-checks shopping list item
description: When a scanned/committed pending item matches an unchecked Mealie shopping item (token match), check it off automatically.

title: Phase 4: Polish & UX
description: URL handling, mobile experience, docs.
